quizQuestion:
Complete the type 2 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If I had more money, I would travel.
(si + imperfect subjunctive + conditional)
sentenceTranslation: If I had more money, I would travel.
explanation: Si tuviera más dinero, viajaría. Type 2 (hypothetical): si + imperfect subjunctive + conditional simple. Contrasts with type 1 (si tienes... irás) which uses present + future for likely conditions.

correctAnswer: Si fuera más joven, correría más.
acceptableAlternatives: Si fuese más joven, correría más.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 2 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If I were younger, I would run more.
(si + fuera/fuese + conditional)
sentenceTranslation: If I were younger, I would run more.
explanation: Si fuera más joven, correría más. Using fuera (imperfect subj of ser) in a type 2 si-clause. This is a hypothetical about present reality — impossible or unlikely from the speaker's perspective.
targetLanguageWord: Si fuera más joven, correría más.

correctAnswer: Si pudiera elegir, viviría en el campo.
acceptableAlternatives: Si pudiese elegir, viviría en el campo.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 2 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If I could choose, I would live in the countryside.
sentenceTranslation: If I could choose, I would live in the countryside.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 2: si pudiera... — poder (hypothetical choice)
explanation: Si pudiera elegir, viviría en el campo. Pudiera is the imperfect subjunctive of poder (yo/él). Type 2 si-clause showing an unlikely or hypothetical present condition.
targetLanguageWord: Si pudiera elegir, viviría en el campo.

correctAnswer: Si hiciera sol, iríamos a la playa.
acceptableAlternatives: Si hiciese sol, iríamos a la playa.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 2 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If it were sunny, we would go to the beach.
sentenceTranslation: If it were sunny, we would go to the beach.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 2: si hiciera sol... — hacer (weather)
explanation: Si hiciera sol, iríamos a la playa. Hiciera is the imperfect subjunctive of hacer (weather expression). The result clause iríamos is the conditional of ir (nosotros).
targetLanguageWord: Si hiciera sol, iríamos a la playa.

correctAnswer: Si hubiera estudiado más, habría aprobado.
acceptableAlternatives: Si hubiese estudiado más, habría aprobado.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 3 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If I had studied more, I would have passed.
(si + pluperfect subj + conditional perfect)
sentenceTranslation: If I had studied more, I would have passed.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 3: si hubiera estudiado... habría aprobado
explanation: Si hubiera estudiado más, habría aprobado. Type 3 (counterfactual past): si + pluperfect subjunctive + conditional perfect. This expresses a past condition that was NOT fulfilled.
targetLanguageWord: Si hubiera estudiado más, habría aprobado.

correctAnswer: Si hubiera llegado antes, habríamos podido hablar.
acceptableAlternatives: Si hubiese llegado antes, habríamos podido hablar.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 3 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If he had arrived earlier, we could have talked.
sentenceTranslation: If he had arrived earlier, we could have talked.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 3: si hubiera llegado... habríamos podido
explanation: Si hubiera llegado antes, habríamos podido hablar. Type 3 counterfactual: si + pluperfect subj + conditional perfect (habríamos podido = we would have been able to).
targetLanguageWord: Si hubiera llegado antes, habríamos podido hablar.

correctAnswer: Si lo hubiera pedido, te habría ayudado.
acceptableAlternatives: Si lo hubiese pedido, te habría ayudado.
quizQuestion:
Complete the type 3 si-clause: '{___}'
Meaning: If you had asked for it, I would have helped you.
sentenceTranslation: If you had asked for it, I would have helped you.
grammarPointLabel: si-clause type 3: si lo hubiera pedido... habría ayudado
explanation: Si lo hubiera pedido, te habría ayudado. Type 3: si + pluperfect subj + conditional perfect. Hubiera pedido = pluperfect subj of pedir. Habría ayudado = conditional perfect of ayudar.
targetLanguageWord: Si lo hubiera pedido, te habría ayudado.

correctAnswer: Si hubiera estudiado medicina, sería médico ahora.
quizQuestion:
Complete the mixed conditional: '{___}'
Meaning: If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.
(past si-clause + present consequence)
sentenceTranslation: If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.
grammarPointLabel: mixed conditional: si hubiera + past + conditional present consequence
explanation: Si hubiera estudiado medicina, sería médico ahora. Mixed conditional: si + pluperfect subjunctive (past counterfactual) + conditional simple (present consequence). The result is a present state that doesn't exist.
targetLanguageWord: Si hubiera estudiado medicina, sería médico ahora.

quizQuestion:
Para el lunes, {___} el proyecto.
(By Monday, I will have finished the project.)
sentenceTranslation: By Monday, I will have finished the project.
explanation: habré terminado — future perfect (futuro perfecto). Formation: habré/habrás/habrá/habremos/habréis/habrán + past participle. Used when an action will be completed before a specific future moment. Para el lunes is the deadline trigger.

quizQuestion:
Cuando llegues, ya {___}.
(When you arrive, we will have already eaten.)
sentenceTranslation: When you arrive, we will have already eaten.
explanation: habremos comido — future perfect, nosotros form. When + future perfect: the eating will be completed before the arrival. Habremos = nosotros form of haber in future.

quizQuestion:
Para las cinco, {___} de la oficina.
(By five o'clock, he will have left the office.)
sentenceTranslation: By five o'clock, he will have left the office.
explanation: habrá salido — future perfect, él/ella form. Salido is the past participle of salir. Para las cinco + future perfect indicates an action completed by a specified time in the future.

quizQuestion:
Para el fin de año, {___} mucho.
(By the end of the year, I will have learned a lot.)
sentenceTranslation: By the end of the year, I will have learned a lot.
explanation: habré aprendido — future perfect, yo form. Para el fin de año + future perfect shows completion before a future deadline. Habré = yo form of haber in future tense.

quizQuestion:
Con más ayuda, {___} antes.
(With more help, I would have finished sooner.)
sentenceTranslation: With more help, I would have finished sooner.
explanation: habría terminado — conditional perfect (condicional compuesto). Formation: habría/habrías/habría/habríamos/habríais/habrían + past participle. Used for hypothetical completed past actions.

quizQuestion:
Si hubiera tenido tiempo, {___}.
(If I had had time, I would have come.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had had time, I would have come.
grammarPointLabel: conditional perfect: habría + venido (yo) — si-clause type 3 result
explanation: habría venido — conditional perfect. The result clause of a type 3 si-clause uses the conditional perfect. Si hubiera tenido (plup. subj.) + habría venido (cond. perfect). Venido = past participle of venir.

quizQuestion:
{___}, pero no tenía tu número.
(I would have called, but I didn't have your number.)
sentenceTranslation: I would have called, but I didn't have your number.
explanation: habría llamado — conditional perfect used without an explicit si-clause. Pero introduces the reason. This shows a past action that didn't happen due to circumstances.

quizQuestion:
{___} de no ser por la lluvia.
(We would have gone if not for the rain.)
sentenceTranslation: We would have gone if not for the rain.
explanation: habríamos ido — conditional perfect, nosotros form. De no ser por + reason is an alternative conditional structure meaning 'if not for'. Habríamos = nosotros form of habría.

quizQuestion:
Sin su apoyo, no {___} éxito.
(Without his support, I would not have had success.)
sentenceTranslation: Without his support, I would not have had success.
grammarPointLabel: conditional perfect: habría + tenido (yo) — sin + conditional perfect
explanation: habría tenido — conditional perfect. Sin (without) + conditional perfect describes what would have happened without a certain condition. Tenido = past participle of tener.
